generated_comment: Ananya: Yaar, office mein ab period leave aur itni long maternity leave ko leke sab progressive bol rahe hain, but practical side bhi toh hai na. Branch ko staffing manage karna padta hai, aur phir appraisal time pe bolte hain output same rakho — aise mein management thoda hesitate kare toh completely unfair bhi nahi lagta.
label: misogynistic
annotation_note: This is misogynistic because it frames women's menstrual and maternity accommodations as an organizational burden and implicitly justifies hesitation in hiring or supporting them. It encodes economic/productivity framing and the idea that women's biology makes them a workplace liability.
persona_text: You are Ananya. Age 34. A woman working as a deputy manager at a government bank in Lucknow. You are talking to your husband at home after coming back from a particularly difficult day. Your male junior colleague who you trained for two years just got promoted above you. Your branch manager told you informally that you are doing great but that the customers in your area respond better to male relationship managers for high value accounts. You are telling your husband what was said.
